name: Multiple Exploit Types Against Single Destination
notes: Reports a destination host attempting to be exploited using multiple types of attacks from one or more source hosts.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same destination IP more than 10 times
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following Exploit: Destination Vulnerable to Detected Exploit on a Different Port
when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same destination IP more than 10 times, across more than 6 QID within 5 minutes
thresholds: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same destination IP more than 10 times, across more than 6 QID within 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Multiple Exploit Types Against Single Destination on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following Exploit: Destination Vulnerable to Detected Exploit on a Different Port
AND when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same destination IP more than 10 times, across more than 6 QID within 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Multiple Exploit/Malware Types Targeting a Single Destination
Event description: Detected a destination attempting to be exploited using multiple types of attacks from one or more sources.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Destination IP
Include detected events by "Destination IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should not contribute to the naming of the associated offense(s)
